content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Minimum Window Substring ". Refer to LeetCode description: https://leetcode.com/problems/minimum-window-substring/
constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain two strings: s and t. The string s will be the string to search for the minimum window substring, and the string t will be the string containing the characters that must be present in the minimum window substring.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the minimum window substring of s that contains all characters of t. If no such window exists, the output will be an empty string.

solution.approach: Use a sliding window approach to find the minimum window substring of s that contains all characters of t.

code.Python3:
from collections import defaultdict

def minWindow(s: str, t: str) -> str:
    if len(s) < len(t): return ""
    tCount = defaultdict(int)
    for c in t: tCount[c] += 1
    required = len(tCount)
    l = r = 0
    formed = 0
    windowCounts = defaultdict(int)
    ans = [-1, 0, 0]
    while r < len(s):
        c = s[r]
        windowCounts[c] += 1
        if c in tCount and windowCounts[c] <= tCount[c]:
            formed += 1
        while l <= r and formed == required:
            c = s[l]
            if ans[0] == -1 or r - l + 1 < ans[0]:
                ans = [r - l + 1, l, r]
            windowCounts[s[l]] -= 1
            if s[l] in tCount and windowCounts[s[l]] < tCount[s[l]]:
                formed -= 1
            l += 1
        r += 1
    return "" if ans[0] == -1 else s[ans[1]:ans[2] + 1]
